news.summary: In Dublin, a report titled "U.S. Personalized Gifting Market - Industry Outlook & Forecast 2025-2030" has been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. The U.S. Personalized Gifting Market achieved a value of USD 9.69 Billion in 2024 and is anticipated to escalate to USD 14.56 Billion by 2030, marking a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 7.02%. This market in the U.S. is fiercely competitive, encompassing various participants, including renowned brands and specialized small businesses, all providing unique offerings.
news.headline: Report predicts U.S. Personalized Gifting market to reach $14.56B by 2030 from $9.69B in 2024, growing at 7.02% CAGR. Industry has a variety of players competing.

news.summary: Senate Banking Committee Chairman Tim Scott criticized the failure of the GENIUS Act in a Senate vote, blaming partisan politics for hindering progress towards greater affordability and innovation in stablecoins. Scott accused Democrats of prioritizing political gamesmanship and obstructing innovation in favor of opposing President Donald Trump. Democrats, including Senator Elizabeth Warren, raised concerns about potential corruption linked to Trump family-backed stablecoin, USD1, alleging a surge in market value due to a shady crypto deal with the UAE. Democrats sponsored bills to address concerns around Trump's crypto activities, with initiatives like the MEME Act and the End Crypto Corruption Act introduced to prevent federal officials from profiting from crypto assets. Scott and Republicans viewed the failure of the GENIUS Act as a missed bipartisan achievement, while Democrats emphasized the need to combat corruption and conflicts of interest in crypto ventures associated with Trump.
news.headline: Senator Tim Scott criticized partisan politics for sabotaging the GENIUS Act, which failed to reach cloture in the Senate for greater affordability and innovation. Scott blamed Democrats for shifting opposition towards the bill to deny President Trump a legislative victory. Senator Elizabeth Warren raised concerns about Trump-linked stablecoin, USD1, citing a surge in market value due to a questionable crypto deal. Democrats introduced bills to address concerns around Trump's crypto activities, with aims to prevent corruption and conflict of interest.

news.summary: A new report on the Belgium Data Center Market has been released by ResearchAndMarkets.com. The market was valued at USD 1.83 billion in 2024 and is expected to reach USD 3.10 billion by 2030, with a projected Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 9.18%. The report provides insights into the market share of data centers in Belgium, analyzing current and future facilities, as well as investments in IT, electrical, and mechanical infrastructure.
news.headline: Belgium Data Center Market valued at USD 1.83B in 2024, projected to reach USD 3.10B by 2030, with a CAGR of 9.18%. Key investors include AtlasEdge, Google, and Proximus.

news.summary: Inuvo, Inc. (NYSE: INUV) recently reported its financial results for the first quarter ending on March 31, 2025. Their revenue reached $26.7 million, exhibiting a substantial 57% surge compared to the previous year, marking it as the highest revenue achievement in the company's history. This exceeded the analyst consensus estimate of $23.7 million. The revenue growth was primarily driven by a striking 61% increase within Platforms and a notable 31% increase within Agencies & Brands. Analysts highlighted Inuvo's alignment with consumer privacy demands and new regulations. A revenue cost of $5.6 million was incurred during this period. For more details, the full story is accessible on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Inuvo Inc. reports 57% revenue growth to $26.7 million in Q1 2025; margin shrinks due to product mix.

news.summary: Over two-thirds (66%) of patients with scalp psoriasis and more than three-quarters (77%) with genital psoriasis who received treatment with the experimental drug icotrokinra successfully achieved clear or almost clear skin at Week 16. Icotrokinra has consistently shown remarkable results in clearing skin issues, with a noteworthy percentage of patients reaching a skin clearance rating of 0 or 1 on the Investigator Global Assessment scale by the specified time point.
news.headline: Icotrokinra shows 66% scalp psoriasis and 77% genital psoriasis patients achieve clear skin at Week 16.

news.summary: Headquartered in Reykjavik, Iceland, on May 9, 2025, Alvotech (NASDAQ: ALVO), a renowned biotech firm focusing on biosimilar medicines development, will attend the BofA Securities Healthcare Conference 2025 in Las Vegas, Nevada from May 13-15, 2025. The company's executives will hold individual meetings during the event and also engage in a fireside chat on May 14, 2025, from 11:40 am-12:10 pm EDT (15:40-16:10 GMT / 17:40-18:10 CET). This participation signifies Alvotech's commitment to engaging with stakeholders and showcasing its dedication to advancing global healthcare.
news.headline: Alvotech to attend BofA Securities Healthcare Conference in Las Vegas on May 13-15, 2025. Fireside chat on May 14 from 11:40am-12:10pm EDT.

news.summary: Alvotech, a leading biotech company listed on NASDAQ under the symbol ALVO, has disclosed its involvement in the upcoming BofA Securities Healthcare Conference 2025, scheduled to take place in Las Vegas, Nevada from May 13 to May 15, 2025. news.summary: In Vancouver, British Columbia, on May 9, 2025, SHARC International Systems Inc. (CSE: SHRC) (FSE: IWIA) (OTCQB: INTWF) shared news that two SHARC 880 Wastewater Energy Transfer ("WET") systems will energize a district energy system in Ottawa, Ontario. This district energy system will support the revitalization of LeBreton Flats and enhance sustainability in the Canadian capital. The SHARC 880 systems are anticipated to play a crucial role in this initiative by leveraging wastewater energy to provide power efficiently.
news.headline: SHARC Energy to power LeBreton Flats redevelopment in Ottawa with 2 WET systems, enhancing district energy.

news.summary: The RMAT designation has been granted based on findings from three clinical trials showcasing the potential advantages of AAV-GAD as a single-dose therapy for Parkinson's disease.
news.headline: MeiraGTx receives FDA RMAT designation for AAV-GAD in treating Parkinson’s disease after 3 successful clinical studies.

news.summary: On May 9, 2025, Ryman Hospitality Properties revealed the approval of a cash dividend for the second quarter. Shareholders of record as of June 30, 2025, will receive $1.15 per share of common stock on July 15, 2025. The Board of Directors at the Company, listed as RHP on NYSE, announced this decision.
news.headline: Ryman Hospitality Properties, Inc. declares $1.15 per share second quarter dividend, to be paid on July 15, 2025.

news.summary: NextNav Inc., a leader in positioning, navigation, and timing (PNT) technology, announced financial results for the quarter ended March 31, 2025. Their revenue increased to $1.5 million from $1.0 million in the prior year period. Operating loss was $17.0 million, up from $16.2 million, primarily due to higher expenses. Net loss was $58.6 million compared to $31.6 million, with $150.4 million in cash and cash equivalents as of March 31, 2025. The FCC's bipartisan vote to explore PNT solutions, including NextNav's, was highlighted. Appointed Rear Admirals H. Wyman Howard and Lorin Selby to the board. NextNav emphasized the importance of a terrestrial PNT solution for national security. They will host a conference call on May 9, 2025, with further details available on their investor relations website. NextNav focuses on driving growth in 3D geolocation and PNT technologies, with risks and uncertainties outlined. Visit their website for more information.
news.headline: NextNav Inc. reported Q1 2025 results, including $1.5 million in revenue. FCC approved NOI with 4-0 vote. Losses include $58.6 million overall, with $150.4 million cash on hand and $213.1 million long term debt as of March 31, 2025. Board of Directors expands with Rear Admirals appointment.

news.summary: Ocugen Inc. reported successful progress in their OCU400 Phase 3 clinical trial for retinitis pigmentosa (RP) and anticipates potential BLA/MAA filings by mid-2026. They also plan to initiate the OCU410ST Phase 2/3 pivotal confirmatory clinical trial for Stargardt disease by mid-2025. The Phase 3 trial for OCU400 includes all eligible RP patients, aiming to treat all 300,000 RP patients in the U.S. and EU. They received FDA alignment for the OCU410ST trial for Stargardt disease affecting 100,000 people in the U.S. and EU. Ocugen's modifier gene therapies for RP, Stargardt disease, and geographic atrophy were granted Advanced Therapy Medicinal Product (ATMP) classification by the EMA. In Q1 2025, they had $38.1 million in cash, with a total of 292.0 million shares outstanding. Operating expenses were $16.0 million, with a net loss of $0.05 per common share. The company plans to discuss these financial results and milestones in a webcast today at 8:30 a.m. ET.
news.headline: Ocugen reported Q1 2025 financial results, $38.1M cash on hand, 292M shares outstanding. OCU400 Phase 3 RP trial progressing, 100% improvement. OCU410ST Phase 2/3 for Stargardt disease initiated soon. OCU410 completes Phase 2. Conference call scheduled at 8:30 am ET. Key programs advancing towards commercialization, multiple regulatory milestones met. 3Q 2025 net loss ($0.05/share). For more details: www.ocugen.com.

news.summary: On May 9, 2025, Dr. Edward Robb, the Chief Strategy Officer at Anivive Lifesciences, delivered a groundbreaking research update on Valley Fever at the 2025 World Vaccine Congress in Washington, D.C. The presentation, entitled "A Double-Edged Sword: Obtaining NIH Funding to Progress," highlighted key findings and advancements in the field. The event saw a large audience with notable attendance from prominent figures in the industry. This marked a significant milestone in the fight against Valley Fever, with Dr. Robb's presentation shedding light on important developments in the quest for a vaccine. The congress featured discussions on various pressing health issues and innovative approaches, underscoring the importance of collaborative efforts in the medical community. Dr. Robb's insights garnered widespread attention, emphasizing the urgency and significance of advancing research in tackling infectious diseases.
news.headline: Anivive presented Valley Fever vaccine research at World Vaccine Congress 2025 by Dr. Edward Robb, Chief Strategy Officer.

news.summary: On May 9, 2025, based in Cambridge, Massachusetts, MetaVia Inc. (Nasdaq: MTVA), a clinical-stage biotechnology corporation specializing in addressing cardiometabolic diseases, revealed a private placement initiative. MetaVia forecasts the placement to generate around $10.0 million in gross proceeds.
news.headline: MetaVia Inc. announces $10.0 million private placement at Nasdaq, focusing on cardiometabolic diseases.

news.summary: SelectQuote, Inc., a prominent Medicare insurance policies distributor and owner of a fast-expanding healthcare services platform, based in Overland Park, Kansas, has expressed its strong disagreement with the claims made in the Department of Justice (DoJ) complaint against several entities in the Medicare Advantage industry. The DoJ complaint, filed last week, accused multiple participants in the industry of wrongdoing. SelectQuote firmly believes that these allegations are unfounded and indicate a misunderstanding of the industry as a whole and the company's business model. SelectQuote's stock is listed on the NYSE under the ticker symbol SLQT.
news.headline: SelectQuote (SLQT) disputes DoJ allegations in Medicare Advantage lawsuit, claims lack of industry knowledge.
